title: M04 Mastery Check — Functions, Scope & Closures
description: Quick applied check. No looking things up. Answer from memory.

questions.prompt: Write a makeAdder(n) factory function that returns a function adding n to any number.
An outer function can access variables declared inside a nested inner function.
questions.explanation: Scope chains flow outward only. Inner functions can access outer scope, but not the reverse.

questions.prompt: Which function type is fully hoisted and callable before its declaration line?
questions.answer_key: Function declarations — function foo() {}. NOT function expressions or arrow functions.

questions.prompt: Explain in one sentence what a closure is.
questions.answer_key: A closure is a function that retains access to variables from its lexical scope even after that scope has exited.
